runs.label: internal strict addition
runs.interpretation: Diagnostic only; addition-only improves deficit but worsens surplus relative to B0.

runs.label: strict add-delete cap 250
runs.mode: strict internal add then capped safe delete
runs.interpretation: Diagnostic only; capped deletion improved deficit and composition surplus but did not beat B0 total surplus.

runs.label: internal full deletion pass
runs.mode: strict internal add then full safe delete
runs.interpretation: Best observed C6 configuration under the tested strict sweep; marginal observed-only variant, not automatically final.

runs.label: semi-internal bounded
runs.mode: semi-internal bounded add then capped safe delete
runs.interpretation: Diagnostic only; improves deficit but worsens surplus and introduces new entities, making it less clean than C6-C.

best_observed_c6_configuration.reason: C6-C is the only tested C6 run that improves both total surplus and total deficit versus B0 while preserving weak component count 1, 139/139 relation coverage, duplicate-free status, and the original B0 entity universe.
best_observed_c6_configuration.claim_boundary: C6-C is the best observed C6 run, but only marginally improves allocation metrics; it is not final, not globally optimal, and not a synthetic completion branch.

symmetric_reverse_audit.conclusion: observed frozen reverse-completion evidence is insufficient; C6 cannot solve symmetric deficit under observed-only frozen constraints

safe_claims: C6 infrastructure can perform controlled observed canonical additions and safe deletion.
C6-C is the best observed C6 run in this sweep.
C6-C modestly improves B0 surplus and deficit while preserving weak component count 1 and relation coverage 139/139.
Observed frozen reverse-completion evidence is insufficient for C6 to solve the symmetric deficit.
C6 does not solve exact balance.
unsafe_claims: C6-C is the final benchmark KG.
C6 proves no better candidate exists.
C6 tested H4 labelled rule-completion for underfilled verified patterns.

planned_h4_subcases.name: symmetric_reverse_completion
planned_h4_subcases.reason: largest directly audited missing reverse-completion bottleneck

planned_h4_subcases.name: inverse_pair_completion
planned_h4_subcases.reason: requires verified inverse-pair confidence and separate false-positive controls

planned_h4_subcases.name: composition_shortcut_completion
planned_h4_subcases.reason: higher-risk shortcut inference; requires rule confidence and conservative labelling

planned_h4_subcases.name: combined_rule_completion_then_safe_delete
planned_h4_subcases.reason: tests whether labelled rule-completion creates enough redundancy to safely remove surplus edges while preserving connectivity and relation coverage

next_actions: Commit C6 infrastructure/code only, if not already committed.
Do not promote C6-C unless a separate human artifact review explicitly decides to preserve and register it.
Open H4 labelled rule-completion for underfilled verified patterns if synthetic or rule-derived edges are allowed, starting with symmetric reverse-completion as the first and safest subcase.
Update docs/reconstruction/77_pre_kge_branch_status_lock.md only if a future candidate decision or H4 plan changes the branch map.
If any C6-derived graph is used for KGE, lock KG artifact path/hash and KGE protocol first.
